seo.description: Decision pages covering custom vs SaaS, contract developer vs agency, Next.js vs SPA, and rewrite vs incremental improvement.

page.title: Comparisons for software buying and delivery decisions | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Comparison pages for buyers making a real software decision
page.description: Decision pages covering custom vs SaaS, contract developer vs agency, Next.js vs SPA, and rewrite vs incremental improvement.
page.primaryQuery: Comparison pages for buyers making a real software decision

hero.title: Decision support when generic internet advice is not enough
hero.subtitle: For buyers who need to compare two realistic paths and understand the impact on delivery, cost, and operations.

intro: These pages are written for practical buying and delivery decisions rather than technical culture-war traffic.
They help with build-vs-buy choices, supplier model evaluation, architecture direction, and the question of rewrite versus incremental improvement.

sections.title: What this section covers
body: Each child page gives a direct answer, shows where option A is stronger, where option B is stronger, and which criteria should shape the decision in a live project.

sections.title: When this section is most useful
body: This section helps a buyer make a better decision before the project gathers momentum in the wrong direction.
bullets: architecture direction for a business application

sections.title: How to continue
body: Start with the child page closest to the current decision and then continue to the relevant service, case study, or inquiry path.
bullets: open the comparison closest to the blocked decision
use it as a frame for internal alignment or budget discussion
continue into the relevant service or inquiry path once direction is clearer

faq.question: Are the comparison pages neutral at all costs?
faq.answer: No. They are practical and explicit about where each option is stronger.

faq.question: Are they useful for non-technical buyers too?
faq.answer: Yes. The framing is built around operational impact, risk, and economics rather than framework tribalism.

faq.question: Do they connect to commercial next steps?
faq.answer: Yes. Every page links onward to the relevant services, supporting content, and inquiry flow.

for: architecture direction for a business application
notFor: passive reading with no live project context

cta.label: Discuss your project
cta.note: If this section matches a live project decision, a short summary is enough to continue.
